title: Checkpoint timeout on StreamOps Flink processor
summary: Flink checkpoints are failing with timeout. Last successful checkpoint was 5 minutes ago; current attempt has been running for 45s (threshold: 30s). Risk of data loss if the job restarts without a valid checkpoint.
root_cause: RocksDB state backend compaction is stalled under write amplification. The state size has grown beyond what the current compaction settings can handle within the checkpoint interval.
timeline: T+0s: Checkpoint timeout scenario injected by simulator
T+10s: MetricGenerator begins emitting checkpoint_duration_ms values in 30000-60000ms range
T+20s: Error logs show checkpoint coordinator timeout warnings
T+25s: Agent detects checkpoint failure via get_checkpoint_stats
T+30s: Diagnostic Agent correlates with state backend metrics and GC pressure
T+35s: Report Agent classifies as CRITICAL due to data loss risk

recommended_actions.rationale: Reduces write amplification by allowing larger SST files, speeding up compaction
Only diffs are snapshotted instead of full state, dramatically reducing checkpoint size

recommended_actions.action: Trigger a manual savepoint before making changes
recommended_actions.rationale: Preserves current state in case the configuration changes cause unexpected behavior

monitoring_notes: After config changes and restart, monitor checkpoint_duration_ms (should stay under 10s), checkpoint_size_bytes, and rocksdb_compaction_pending. Alert if checkpoint duration exceeds 20s again within the first hour.
